channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Atmospheric river brings weather whiplash to East Coast as bomb cyclone develops
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The U.S. East Coast began a whiplash-inducing stretch of weather Wednesday with a deluge of rain, rapid snowmelt and powerful gusts, creating dangerous conditions, due in part to an atmospheric river and developing bomb cyclone.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says healthy women help US prosperity as he highlights White House initiative on their health
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Wednesday that he is “so proud” that a women's health research initiative he launched last year at his wife's urging has already invested nearly $1 billion because a healthy female population improves U.S. prosperity.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gun found on suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO matches shell casings at scene, police say
item.description: ALTOONA, Pa. (AP) — The gun found on the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare’s CEO matched shell casings found at the site of the shooting, New York City’s police commissioner said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Secretary of State Blinken is returning to the Mideast in his latest diplomatic foray
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken is returning to the Middle East this week on his 12th visit since the Israel-Hamas war erupted last year but his first since the ouster of Syrian President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The man accused of killing the head of one of the biggest U.S. health insurance companies is fighting being sent to face charges in New York City, where the attack happened.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Christopher Wray to resign as FBI director ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced Wednesday he plans to resign from the agency, leaving his post ahead of President-Trump's apparent plans to fire him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey drones: NORTHCOM not yet asked by local officials for help
item.description: Local officials have not yet requested that U.S. Northern Command intervene amid reports of mysterious drone sightings over New Jersey and Staten Island on the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luxury real estate brokers charged in federal indictment with sex trafficking in NYC
item.description: Two luxury real estate brokers and their brother have been charged in a federal indictment with sex trafficking.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alex Jones keeps Infowars for now after judge rejects The Onion's winning auction bid
item.description: A federal judge in Texas rejected the auction sale of Alex Jones’ Infowars to The Onion satirical news outlet, criticizing the bidding for the conspiracy theory platform as flawed as well as how much money families of the 2012 Sandy Hook Elementary shooting stood to receive.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Homeland Security shares new details of mysterious drone flights over New Jersey, lawmaker says
item.description: A New Jersey lawmaker from part of the state where several mysterious drones have been spotted in recent week says the devices appear to avoid detection by traditional methods.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Shipkowski, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bolton: Senators ‘won’t escape history’s judgment’ if they vote to confirm Patel to FBI
item.description: John Bolton, President-elect Trump’s former national security adviser, is warning that Republican senators will be judged harshly by history if they confirm Kash Patel as the next FBI director.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Dick Van Dyke, Cher among those forced to flee Malibu homes in wake of wildfire
item.description: Officials said the fire, which grew significantly overnight, was only 7% contained as of Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio lawmaker proposes bill to make ‘flag planting’ in Ohio State stadium a felony
item.description: Days after the postgame melee between the Michigan and Ohio State football teams, an Ohio state lawmaker has introduced a new bill that would make “flag planting” within Ohio Stadium a felony criminal offense.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KRON4

item.title: Luigi Mangione's fingerprints match water bottle found near shooting: sources
item.description: Fingerprints taken from Luigi Mangione in Pennsylvania matched those on a water bottle found near the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's fatal shooting, sources said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Albertsons pulls the plug on merger with Kroger
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons in 2022 proposed what would be the largest grocery store merger in U.S. history. The companies said a merger would help them better compete with big retailers like Walmart, Costco and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Mega Millions winner sues for other half of $394M jackpot
item.description: A man is suing the California Lottery, claiming he bought both winning tickets for a $394 million Mega Millions jackpot but lost one and is owed the remaining $197 million.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Albertsons gives up on Kroger merger, sues the grocery chain
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons in 2022 proposed what would be the largest grocery store merger in U.S. history. The companies said a merger would help them better compete with big retailers like Walmart, Costco and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The burial site of the people Andrew Jackson enslaved was lost. The Hermitage says it is found
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — At least 26 enslaved people died on the Tennessee plantation of President Andrew Jackson between 1804 and the end of the Civil War in 1865. Where they were laid to rest is knowledge that had been lost to time.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inflation ticks higher as Fed eyes another rate cut
item.description: Inflation picked up slightly in November, according to data released Wednesday by the Labor Department, but likely not by enough to derail the Federal Reserve's plans for another rate cut.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KRON4

item.title: Sanders says this will likely be his last term
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), one of the Senate’s most prominent progressives, says he likely won’t run again for the Senate after his fourth term expires in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Luigi Mangione was reported missing 2 weeks before CEO slain: police
item.description: The parents of Luigi Mangione, the man suspected of shooting and killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, told law enforcement that they hadn't heard from their son in months.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Nicole Johnson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kylie Kelce dethrones Joe Rogan on podcast charts as her debut episode hits No. 1
item.description: Kylie Kelce, wife of former NFL player turned media personality Jason Kelce, beat out Joe Rogan for the top podcast spot on both Apple and Spotify this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of Biden's pardon of his son Hunter, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of President Joe Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter after earlier promising he would do no such thing, according to a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Increased Christianity in schools opens the door to Satanic Temple education programs
item.description: The in-school offerings are in addition to the After School Satan Clubs, which launched in 2020 and have risen in popularity among parents seeking alternatives to religious clubs.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Las Vegas man who jumped a desk to attack judge in courtroom sentenced to at least 26 years in prison
item.description: A Las Vegas man who was seen on video jumping over a courtroom bench and desk and attacking a judge was sentenced to at least 26 years behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: San Diego sheriff defies new policy to limit cooperation with immigration officials
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The sheriff of the nation’s fifth-largest county on Tuesday defied a new policy to limit cooperation with federal immigration authorities, setting up a showdown over a new obstacle to President-elect Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect intends to plead not guilty: lawyer
item.description: Alleged UnitedHealthcare CEO shooter Luigi Mangione was arrested at a Pennsylvania McDonald's on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: After acquittal in subway chokehold trial, Daniel Penny says he was vulnerable in the encounter
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After being acquitted of homicide, the military veteran who choked a volatile, mentally ill man on a New York subway told an interviewer he put himself in a “very vulnerable position” but felt compelled to act.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and SANYA MANSOOR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mega Millions winner sues for other half of $394M jackpot, claims he bought both tickets
item.description: A man is suing the California Lottery, claiming he bought both winning tickets for a $394 million Mega Millions jackpot but lost one and is owed the remaining $197 million.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Wisconsin kayaker who faked his death and fled the country is charged after returning to US
item.description: Authorities say a Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning and left his wife and three children for Eastern Europe has willingly returned to the U.S. Ryan Borgwardt is charged with obstruction, a misdemeanor.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Joe Burrow's home broken into during Monday Night Football
item.description: Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into during Monday Night Football in the latest home invasion of a pro athlete in the U.S., authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KTLA 5

item.title: Making a $1B investment in the US? Trump pledges expedited permits — but there are hurdles
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is promising expedited federal permits for energy projects and other construction worth more than $1 billion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
